# ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0019 - QUIC RFC 9000 NEW_TOKEN ACK Piggyback Topoff Architecture

## Purpose

Describe the bounded explicit-path 1-RTT NEW_TOKEN packet-composition topoff that reuses the Application Data ACK piggyback policy when a server emits a token after validating a client address.

## Scope

This design covers pending Application Data ACK piggybacking on server NEW_TOKEN packets emitted through the explicit-path 1-RTT application send helper. It does not claim Initial or Handshake ACK piggybacking, a general delayed-ACK timer, endpoint interop success, or broader multipath scheduling.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0005
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0011
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0010

## Design Summary

The explicit-path 1-RTT application send helper, currently used by server NEW_TOKEN emission after path validation, performs the same transient ACK-prefix composition used by the active-path stream send helper. When Application Data ACK state is pending, the helper protects ACK plus NEW_TOKEN in one Application Data packet, marks the ACK frame as sent under the carrier packet number, and keeps the original NEW_TOKEN payload as the retransmittable plaintext. If no ACK is pending, the helper emits the NEW_TOKEN payload unchanged.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.Streams.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0010.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/QuicS13AckPiggybackTestSupport.cs

## Data and State Considerations

The topoff does not add a second ACK scheduler or a path-selection policy. It reuses the existing Application Data ACK-generation state and the existing server-only token-emission table. ACK bytes are transient packet-composition data; the sent-packet retransmission payload remains the original NEW_TOKEN frame bytes.

## Edge Cases and Constraints

- Only explicit-path 1-RTT NEW_TOKEN packet composition is in scope.
- No ACK frame is invented when ACK-generation state has no pending Application Data receipt.
- A piggybacked ACK frame is recorded under the actual protected packet number so duplicate ACK-only sends for the same trigger remain suppressed.
- Server-only NEW_TOKEN emission and per-address deduplication remain owned by ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0002.

## Alternatives Considered

- Widen ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0018 after the fact; rejected because the active-path stream-send slice was intentionally committed with a narrower boundary.
- Treat all candidate-path application sends as covered; rejected because the executable seam proven here is the explicit-path NEW_TOKEN helper only.
- Delay this until Initial and Handshake packet-builder piggybacking; rejected because NEW_TOKEN uses the existing 1-RTT Application Data helper and can be proven independently.

## Risks

- Initial and Handshake ACK piggyback policy remains unclaimed.
- General candidate-path or multipath scheduling remains unclaimed beyond the server NEW_TOKEN explicit-path topoff.
- Endpoint interop success and public API behavior remain outside this architecture slice.
